# atteler

> Go-only LLM harness: a TUI/CLI over multiple providers (anthropic, claude-code,
> codex, openai, ollama) with sessions, agents, hooks, worktrees, and multi-agent runs.

This file is generated. `llms-full.txt` contains the full flattened documentation.

## Documentation
- [Atteler](docs/index.md): A Go-only LLM harness: a Bubble Tea TUI and a large CLI over multiple providers, with sessions, agents, lifecycle hooks, git-worktree isolation, and multi-agent speculative/review runs.
- [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md): Build atteler, run it from a clone, and explore the command surface offline.
- [Common workflows](docs/common-workflows.md): Task-oriented recipes for everyday Atteler runs, from one-shot chat to multi-agent review.
- [Configuration](docs/configuration.md): Layered YAML/JSON configuration, generation knobs, and the routing/agent schema.
- [Providers](docs/providers.md): The built-in LLM providers and how authentication resolves.
- [Hooks](docs/hooks.md): Lifecycle events you can subscribe to from YAML.
- [CLI reference](docs/cli-reference.md): The complete, generated command surface.
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): An orientation map of how Atteler is put together: a Go-only LLM harness with a thin CLI/TUI shell over reusable, stateless library packages.
- [Lifecycle hook events](docs/lifecycle-events.md): Lifecycle hook payloads use `schema_version: 1` and are filtered through the selected `payload` policy before local hooks receive them.
- [Symphony](docs/symphony.md): Atteler includes a standalone `symphony` command that implements the Symphony service contract from OpenAI's draft specification.
